tokenEstimate.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

environmentParity.runner: Selected with --runner; vanilla and fooks variants use the same runner.
environmentParity.prompt: Identical task text for vanilla and fooks variants.
environmentParity.codexHome: Each variant uses an isolated CODEX_HOME with auth.json and config.toml symlinked from the same host account.
environmentParity.variantDifference: The fooks variant either runs fooks init/scan/attach before the same agent command or records an exact-file first-turn bypass when no fooks context would be injected; vanilla does not.

scale.critical: Do not cite without fixing measurement.

risks.name: sample_size
risks.interpretation: Use as round-1 proof only; repeat N>=3 before claiming stable performance.

risks.name: environment_parity
risks.interpretation: Both variants use the same selected runner with isolated CODEX_HOME; fooks may prepare native context or explicitly bypass exact-file first-turn preparation.

risks.name: orchestration_overhead
risks.interpretation: Direct Codex is the cleaner product benchmark; OMX runner includes orchestration overhead and policy surface.

risks.name: cleanup_reproducibility
risks.interpretation: Worktree residue can contaminate reruns if cleanup fails.

risks.name: wall_clock_noise
risks.evidence: Single-run wall-clock timing includes model/service variability.
risks.interpretation: Prefer median and p95 over repeated identical tasks.

risks.name: artifact_quality_scope
risks.interpretation: Do not cite wins unless fooks passes task acceptance and avoids broader edit scope than vanilla.

risks.name: runtime_token_claim
risks.evidence: Actual OMX/Codex tokens available; fooks used more runtime tokens in 0/1 pair(s).
risks.interpretation: Do not claim runtime token reduction from this run; keep proxy compression and actual tokens separate.

tokens.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

vanilla.stdout_tail:
Implemented the Caps Lock warning for the login password field only.

Modified files:
- [login-form.tsx](/Users/veluga/Workspace/fooks-test-repos/.worktrees/bm-T5-vanilla-1776432936/apps/web/modules/auth/login/components/login-form.tsx:148)
- [en-US.json](/Users/veluga/Workspace/fooks-test-repos/.worktrees/bm-T5-vanilla-1776432936/apps/web/locales/en-US.json:58)

What changed:
- Added password-field key handling with `event.getModifierState("CapsLock")`.
- Shows an inline red Tailwind warning below the login password input while Caps Lock is detected.
- Kept the change scoped to the login form, so shared `PasswordInput`, password reset, profile confirmation, and two-factor components are unchanged.
- Added the English translation key `auth.login.caps_lock_warning`.

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Could not run Prettier/ESLint because this checkout has no `node_modules`, and `prettier`/`eslint` were not available.

build.reason: build validation not run for vanilla

fooks.stdout_tail:
Implemented the Caps Lock warning for the login password field.

Modified files:
- [login-form.tsx](/Users/veluga/Workspace/fooks-test-repos/.worktrees/bm-T5-fooks-1776433285/apps/web/modules/auth/login/components/login-form.tsx:148)
- [en-US.json](/Users/veluga/Workspace/fooks-test-repos/.worktrees/bm-T5-fooks-1776433285/apps/web/locales/en-US.json:58)

What changed:
- Added local `isCapsLockOn` state in the login form.
- Added `onKeyDown` / `onKeyUp` handlers on the login `PasswordInput` using `event.getModifierState("CapsLock")`.
- Renders an inline Tailwind red warning below the password field: `text-red-500`.
- Clears the warning on password field blur.
- Added the English translation key `auth.login.caps_lock_warning`.

Verification:
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `apps/web/locales/en-US.json` parses as valid JSON.
- `pnpm exec prettier` and `pnpm exec eslint` could not run because `prettier` and `eslint` are not installed/resolvable in this checkout.
